skill.name: typescript-write
skill.description: Write TypeScript and JavaScript code following Metabase coding standards and best practices. Use when developing or refactoring TypeScript/JavaScript code.
skill.summary: Guide TypeScript and JavaScript changes using Metabase development standards.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged documented Yarn commands and one weak cryptography blocker. Review found normal Metabase development workflow commands and no cryptographic code, hidden execution, network access, secret access, or prompt injection. The skill is safe to publish with a low-risk note because it asks agents to run project test and lint commands.

low_findings.title: Documented Yarn Development Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are visible workflow instructions for linting, formatting, type checking, and tests. They are not hidden shell execution, but they can run project package scripts in the local repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented as Metabase lint, format, type check, and test commands. They still execute repository-defined Yarn scripts, so a low-risk warning is appropriate.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: The blocker finding points to the skill description line. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, cipher, or crypto API appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain front matter describing TypeScript and JavaScript development. There is direct evidence that the weak cryptography finding does not match the file contents.

content.value_statement: Metabase contributors need code changes that match existing TypeScript and JavaScript standards. This skill guides implementation, tests, linting, formatting, and type checks in the expected workflow.
actual_capabilities: Guides TypeScript and JavaScript development for the Metabase codebase.
Encourages failing tests before implementation changes.
Directs use of ESLint, Prettier, and TypeScript checks.
Lists commands for unit tests, pattern-based tests, and ClojureScript tests.
Promotes small, testable increments and existing code patterns.
limitations: It does not provide detailed Metabase architecture documentation.
It does not define new coding standards beyond the listed workflow.
It depends on the project repository having the referenced Yarn scripts.
It does not automate commits or release preparation.

use_cases.title: Add a Tested UI Fix
use_cases.description: Implement a focused TypeScript or JavaScript change with a failing test first, then run targeted verification.

use_cases.title: Refactor Existing Code
use_cases.description: Improve code structure while preserving behavior and checking style, formatting, and types.

use_cases.title: Verify a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Run the expected lint, format, type check, and test commands before review.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Small Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to update this TypeScript or JavaScript feature. Add or update a targeted test first, then make the smallest working change.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task with a clear target file

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Failing Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to investigate this failing unit test. Explain the failing behavior, update the implementation, and run the targeted test command.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor With Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to refactor this JavaScript or TypeScript module. Preserve behavior, follow nearby patterns, and run lint, formatting, and type checks.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Review-Ready Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to complete this Metabase code change. Work in small increments, cover tests, run targeted checks, and summarize risks for review.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow before pull request review

output_examples.input: Add validation for a TypeScript form field.
output_examples.output: A targeted test is added first, the validation logic is implemented, and the relevant unit test, lint, and type checks are reported.

output_examples.input: Refactor a JavaScript utility used by several components.
output_examples.output: The utility keeps the same behavior, nearby call sites are reviewed, formatting is applied, and focused tests are run.
The expected ClojureScript test command is identified and run with a short summary of pass or fail results.

best_practices: Start with the smallest failing test that proves the requested behavior.
Study nearby code patterns before introducing new structure.
Run targeted tests and checks after each meaningful change.
anti_patterns: Do not make broad refactors unrelated to the requested change.
Do not skip tests when behavior changes are expected.
Do not edit files outside the project folder.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents write and verify TypeScript and JavaScript code using Metabase development standards.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It lists Yarn commands that an agent may run during development. Users should review command execution in untrusted repositories.

faq.question: Is this only for Metabase?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow and commands are specific to the Metabase project.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for failing tests first and lists commands for unit tests and ClojureScript tests.

faq.question: Does it change Git history?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly tells agents not to commit changes.

faq.question: Can it replace project documentation?
faq.answer: No. It is a compact workflow guide and should be used with the repository documentation and existing code.
